title: fritillaria lusitanica f. stenophylla
intro: Fritillaria lusitanica f. stenophylla, also known as the Portuguese snake's head, is a bulbous perennial plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving cough and sore throat, and treating respiratory infections, due to its expectorant and mucolytic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's bulbs can be dried and used to make teas, infusions, and tonics, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies and folk medicine practices.
